channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Build an Agent That Thinks Like a Data Scientist: How We Hit #1 on DABStep with Reusable Tool Generation
Network Performance Issues in Russia
item.description: Mar 12, 23:56 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 12, 23:56 UTCUpdate - We are continuing to investigate this issue.Mar 12, 23:31 UTCUpdate - We are continuing to investigate this issue.Mar 12, 20:08 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating issues with network performance in...

item.title: AI Gateway
item.description: Mar 12, 23:15 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 12, 23:07 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Mar 12, 22:09 UTCInvestigating - Prepaid billing unable to purchase credits.

item.title: Retrieval After RAG: Hybrid Search, Agents, and Database Design — Simon Hørup Eskildsen of Turbopuffer
item.description: Turbopuffer came out of a reading app.

item.title: A defense official reveals how AI chatbots could be used for targeting decisions
item.description: The US military might use generative AI systems to rank lists of targets and make recommendations—which would be vetted by humans—about which to strike first, according to a Defense Department official with knowledge of the matter. The disclosure about how the military may use AI chatbots comes as...

item.title: Fix for Bot Management Cookie Issue with Cloudflare for SaaS (O2O) Deployments
item.description: Mar 12, 20:40 UTCResolved - Cloudflare is deploying a fix for a bug affecting customers using Bot Management with Cloudflare for SaaS origin-to-origin (O2O) configurations. These customers may have observed an issue where the __cf_bm cookie was repeatedly being reset on each O2O request. This...

item.title: INC0153465
item.description: Mar 12, 20:18 UTCUpdate - Current status: We're continuing to monitor as the rollback of the Snowflake Intelligence update progresses. Affected customers should continue to experience relief as the rollback advances and reaches their environment. We'll provide another update within 4 hours.Customer...

item.title: Some users seeing "No Accessible Workspaces" when attempting to login via SSO
item.description: Status: MonitoringWe have applied the mitigation and are monitoring the recovery.Affected components Login (Degraded performance)

item.title: Rust-Based VENON Malware Targets 33 Brazilian Banks with Credential-Stealing Overlays
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new banking malware targeting Brazilian users that's written in Rust, marking a significant departure from other known Delphi-based malware families associated with the Latin American cybercrime ecosystem. The malware, which is designed to...

item.title: Hive0163 Uses AI-Assisted Slopoly Malware for Persistent Access in Ransomware Attacks
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a suspected artificial intelligence (AI)-generated malware codenamed Slopoly put to use by a financially motivated threat actor named Hive0163. "Although still relatively unspectacular, AI-generated malware such as Slopoly shows how easily threat...

item.title: Increased Error Rates in North America West
item.description: Mar 12, 16:46 UTCUpdate - We are continuing to investigate this issue.Mar 12, 16:45 UTCInvestigating - Users may experience increased error rates when interacting with Cloudflare services in the Seattle region.

item.title: Cloudchamber Service Degradation
item.description: Mar 12, 15:36 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.Mar 12, 14:22 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating an issue causing a degradation in the Cloudchamber service, which is preventing some customers from provisioning new containers. Our engineering...

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Sonnet 4.6
item.description: Mar 12, 16:34 UTCIdentified - A repeat of an earlier issue today started at 9:27 PT / 16:27 UTC.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Sonnet 4.6 and on Claude.ai
item.description: Mar 12, 16:28 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 12, 16:07 UTCMonitoring - We recently had an incident on Sonnet 4.6 between 8:30 PT / 15:30 UTC and 8:44 PT / 15:44 UTC where users would have seen a higher error rate across Claude Code, Claude API, and Claude.ai.Separately, we also...

item.title: Degraded Codespaces experience
item.description: Mar 12, 16:09 UTCUpdate - We're seeing intermittent failures downloading from the extension marketplace from codespaces and are investigating.Mar 12, 15:08 UTCUpdate - We're seeing partial recovery for the issue affecting extension installation in newly created Codespaces. Some users may still...

item.title: Elevated errors on Sonnet 4.6
item.description: Mar 12, 15:48 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 12, 15:42 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Apple Updates Legacy iOS Versions to Patch Coruna Exploits
item.description: The company has released iOS and iPadOS versions 16.7.15 and 15.8.7 to patch the vulnerabilities. The post Apple Updates Legacy iOS Versions to Patch Coruna Exploits appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: How to Scale Phishing Detection in Your SOC: 3 Steps for CISOs
item.description: Phishing has quietly turned into one of the hardest enterprise threats to expose early. Instead of crude lures and obvious payloads, modern campaigns rely on trusted infrastructure, legitimate-looking authentication flows, and encrypted traffic that conceals malicious behavior from traditional...

item.title: Meta Launches New Protection Tools as It Helps Disrupt Scam Centers
item.description: The social media giant has disabled more than 150,000 accounts powering scam centers in Asia. The post Meta Launches New Protection Tools as It Helps Disrupt Scam Centers appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: OAuth Trap, EDR Killer, Signal Phishing, Zombie ZIP, AI Platform Hack & More
item.description: Another Thursday, another pile of weird security stuff that somehow happened in just seven days. Some of it is clever. Some of it is lazy. A few bits fall into that uncomfortable category of “yeah… this is probably going to show up in real incidents sooner than we’d like.” The pattern this week...

item.title: The Download: Early adopters cash in on China’s OpenClaw craze, and US batteries slump
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Hustlers are cashing in on China’s OpenClaw AI craze  In January, Beijing-based software engineer Feng Qingyang started tinkering...

item.title: Pragmatic by design: Engineering AI for the real world
item.description: The impact of artificial intelligence extends far beyond the digital world and into our everyday lives, across the cars we drive, the appliances in our homes, and medical devices that keep people alive. More and more, product engineers are turning to AI to enhance, validate, and streamline the...

item.title: Ally WordPress Plugin Flaw Exposes Over 200,000 Websites to Attacks
item.description: The issue allows attackers to inject SQL queries and extract sensitive information from the database. The post Ally WordPress Plugin Flaw Exposes Over 200,000 Websites to Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Human IOC: Why Security Professionals Struggle with Social Vetting
item.description: Applying SOC-level rigor to the rumors, politics, and 'human intel' can make or break a security team. The post The Human IOC: Why Security Professionals Struggle with Social Vetting appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Splunk, Zoom Patch Severe Vulnerabilities
item.description: Critical- and high-severity flaws could be exploited to execute arbitrary shell commands or elevate privileges. The post Splunk, Zoom Patch Severe Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: International security chiefs to convene in Glasgow for flagship CYBERUK conference
item.description: CYBERUK will be delivered by the NCSC and sponsors across four distinct tracks of activity: Resilience, Technology, Threat, and Ecosystem.

item.title: Attackers Don't Just Send Phishing Emails. They Weaponize Your SOC's Workload
item.description: The most dangerous phishing campaigns aren’t just designed to fool employees. Many are designed to exhaust the analysts investigating them. When a phishing investigation takes 12 hours instead of five minutes, the outcome can shift from a contained incident to a breach. For years, the cybersecurity...

item.title: Brutal times for the US battery industry
item.description: Just a few years ago, the battery industry was hot, hot, hot. There was a seemingly infinite number of companies popping up, with shiny new chemistries and massive fundraising rounds. My biggest problem was sifting through the pile to pick the most exciting news to cover. That tide has turned, and...

item.title: AGIBOT Showcases Full Robot Lineup at AWE 2026
item.description: AGIBOT showcased its full robot family—Yuanzheng, Lingxi, Jingling, and Kuo—at AWE 2026, demonstrating humanoid robots performing calligraphy, comedy, and dance with natural interaction.

item.title: Tencent’s WorkBuddy Agent Tool Adds Direct WeChat Integration
item.description: Tencent's AI agent WorkBuddy has been updated to support direct WeChat connectivity, allowing users to remotely command their desktop AI via mobile messages.

item.title: CarbonSeeker’s Consumer Continuous-Fiber 3D Printer Raises $4.7M on Kickstarter
item.description: Shenzhen-based Anisoprint raised over $4.7 million on Kickstarter for its FibreSeeker 3—the world's first consumer continuous carbon fiber 3D printer priced under $3,000.

item.title: Zero One Automotive Raises $170M to Build the “Tesla of Heavy Trucks”
item.description: Zero One Automotive has secured 1.2 billion RMB in new funding to scale its intelligent electric truck production, targeting 5,000 units in 2026 to become the "Tesla of heavy trucks."

item.title: C12.ai Raises $7M to Advance “AI Scientist” Robotics for Drug Discovery
item.description: Shanghai-based AI robotics company C12.ai has raised $7 million in new funding, with its robot scientist "Talos" completing 50 days of zero-error drug purification trials at BeiGene's laboratory.

item.title: [AINews] Replit Agent 4: The Knowledge Work Agent
item.description: Replit Agent 4 lets us reflect on a couple disparate releases.

item.title: Actions failures to download (401 Unauthorized)
item.description: Mar 12, 04:46 UTCInvestigating - We are investigating reports of degraded performance for Actions

item.title: Agentic Control Center for Data Product Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2603.10133v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Data products enable end users to gain greater insights about their data by providing supporting assets, such as example question-SQL pairs which can be answered using the data or views over the database tables. However, producing useful data products...

item.title: Hybrid Self-evolving Structured Memory for GUI Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.10291v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The remarkable progress of vision-language models (VLMs) has enabled GUI agents to interact with computers in a human-like manner. Yet real-world computer-use tasks remain difficult due to long-horizon workflows, diverse interfaces, and frequent...

item.title: HEAL: Hindsight Entropy-Assisted Learning for Reasoning Distillation
item.description: arXiv:2603.10359v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Distilling reasoning capabilities from Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) into smaller models is typically constrained by the limitation of rejection sampling. Standard methods treat the teacher as a static filter, discarding complex "corner-case" problems...

item.title: Beyond Scalars: Evaluating and Understanding LLM Reasoning via Geometric Progress and Stability
item.description: arXiv:2603.10384v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Evaluating LLM reliability via scalar probabilities often fails to capture the structural dynamics of reasoning. We introduce TRACED, a framework that assesses reasoning quality through theoretically grounded geometric kinematics. By decomposing...

item.title: Verbalizing LLM's Higher-order Uncertainty via Imprecise Probabilities
item.description: arXiv:2603.10396v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Despite the growing demand for eliciting uncertainty from large language models (LLMs), empirical evidence suggests that LLM behavior is not always adequately captured by the elicitation techniques developed under the classical probabilistic...

item.title: Explainable LLM Unlearning Through Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2603.09980v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM unlearning is essential for mitigating safety, copyright, and privacy concerns in pre-trained large language models (LLMs). Compared to preference alignment, it offers a more explicit way by removing undesirable knowledge characterized by specific...

item.title: MoE-SpAc: Efficient MoE Inference Based on Speculative Activation Utility in Heterogeneous Edge Scenarios
item.description: arXiv:2603.09983v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models enable scalable performance but face severe memory constraints on edge devices. Existing offloading strategies struggle with I/O bottlenecks due to the dynamic, low-information nature of autoregressive expert...

item.title: Personalized Group Relative Policy Optimization for Heterogenous Preference Alignment
item.description: arXiv:2603.10009v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Despite their sophisticated general-purpose capabilities, Large Language Models (LLMs) often fail to align with diverse individual preferences because standard post-training methods, like Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback (RLHF), optimize for...

item.title: LWM-Temporal: Sparse Spatio-Temporal Attention for Wireless Channel Representation Learning
item.description: arXiv:2603.10024v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LWM-Temporal is a new member of the Large Wireless Models (LWM) family that targets the spatiotemporal nature of wireless channels. Designed as a task-agnostic foundation model, LWM-Temporal learns universal channel embeddings that capture...

item.title: Gated Adaptation for Continual Learning in Human Activity Recognition
item.description: arXiv:2603.10046v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Wearable sensors in Internet of Things (IoT) ecosystems increasingly support applications such as remote health monitoring, elderly care, and smart home automation, all of which rely on robust human activity recognition (HAR). Continual learning...

item.title: GhazalBench: Usage-Grounded Evaluation of LLMs on Persian Ghazals
item.description: arXiv:2603.09979v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Persian poetry plays an active role in Iranian cultural practice, where verses by canonical poets such as Hafez are frequently quoted, paraphrased, or completed from partial cues. Supporting such interactions requires language models to engage not...

item.title: Large Language Models and Book Summarization: Reading or Remembering, Which Is Better?
item.description: arXiv:2603.09981v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Summarization is a core task in Natural Language Processing (NLP). Recent advances in Large Language Models (LLMs) and the introduction of large context windows reaching millions of tokens make it possible to process entire books in a single prompt....

item.title: AraModernBERT: Transtokenized Initialization and Long-Context Encoder Modeling for Arabic
item.description: arXiv:2603.09982v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Encoder-only transformer models remain widely used for discriminative NLP tasks, yet recent architectural advances have largely focused on English. In this work, we present AraModernBERT, an adaptation of the ModernBERT encoder architecture to Arabic,...

item.title: An Efficient Hybrid Deep Learning Approach for Detecting Online Abusive Language
item.description: arXiv:2603.09984v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The digital age has expanded social media and online forums, allowing free expression for nearly 45% of the global population. Yet, it has also fueled online harassment, bullying, and harmful behaviors like hate speech and toxic comments across social...

item.title: The Dunning-Kruger Effect in Large Language Models: An Empirical Study of Confidence Calibration
item.description: arXiv:2603.09985v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable capabilities across diverse tasks, yet their ability to accurately assess their own confidence remains poorly understood. We present an empirical study investigating whether LLMs exhibit...
